data.title: FIRST COMMUNITY CORP /SC/ SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
First Community Corp /SC/, a prominent player in the financial services industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report showcases significant improvements in both financial and operational metrics, underscoring the company's robust performance and strategic growth initiatives.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $23.1 million, reflecting a $1.8 million increase from the previous year due to a $126.0 million increase in average earning assets and a 0.34% increase in net interest margin.
  • Net Interest Income: $14.4 million, up by $2.3 million or 19.2% from the previous year, driven by higher average earning assets and improved net interest margin.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: $437,000, primarily due to a $473,000 increase in the allowance for credit losses – loans, offset by a decline in the allowance for credit losses - unfunded commitments.
  • Total Non-Interest Income: $4.0 million, an increase of $798,000, mainly due to higher mortgage banking income and investment advisory fees.
  • Total Non-Interest Expense: $12.8 million, up by $949,000, largely due to increased salaries and employee benefits, equipment costs, and other non-interest expenses.
  • Net Income: $4.0 million, an increase of $1.4 million from the previous year, attributed to higher net interest income and non-interest income.
  • Basic Earnings Per Common Share: $0.52, compared to $0.34 in the previous year, reflecting improved profitability.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $0.51, up from $0.34 in the previous year, indicating enhanced earnings performance.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates through four primary segments: Commercial and Retail Banking, Mortgage Banking, Investment Advisory and Non-Deposit, and Corporate. Each segment contributes differently to the overall business, with Commercial and Retail Banking being the primary driver of deposit and lending products.
  • Mortgage Banking Performance: Mortgage banking income increased significantly, driven by higher production volumes. The segment saw $43.9 million in total production, including $25.8 million in secondary market loans, $4.0 million in adjustable rate mortgages, and $14.1 million in new construction residential real estate loan commitments.
  • Investment Advisory Growth: Investment advisory fees rose by $448,000, reflecting an increase in assets under management, which reached $892.8 million. Despite a decline in the US stock market, the segment managed to attract net new assets of $21.5 million.
  • Sales Units and Production Volumes: The mortgage banking segment reported a substantial increase in production volumes, with a notable rise in secondary market loans and commitments for new construction residential real estate loans.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in its core banking and mortgage segments, supported by strategic initiatives to enhance customer service and expand product offerings. The management remains focused on maintaining a strong balance sheet and capital position to support future growth opportunities.

SEC Filing: FIRST COMMUNITY CORP /SC/ [ FCCO ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Clearfield, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Clearfield, Inc., a leading provider of fiber management, protection, and delivery solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended March 31, 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by increased sales and strategic business initiatives.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: Clearfield, Inc. reported net sales of $47.168 million, marking a 28% increase from $36.910 million for the same period in 2024. This growth was primarily driven by increased sales to Large Regional Service Provider customers and MSO customers.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $14.192 million, representing 30.1% of net sales. This is a significant improvement from $2.832 million or 7.7% of net sales for the same period in 2024, attributed to better utilization of manufacturing overhead and lower excess inventory charges.

Income from Operations: Clearfield, Inc. reported an income from operations of $0.262 million, a notable turnaround from a loss of $9.741 million for the same period in 2024. This improvement reflects increased net sales and a higher gross profit margin.

Net Income: The company posted a net income of $1.327 million, compared to a net loss of $5.911 million for the same period in 2024. The increase in net income is due to higher net sales and an improved gross profit margin.

Net Income Per Share Basic: Basic net income per share was $0.09, compared to a loss of $0.40 per share for the same period in 2024, reflecting the increase in net income.

Net Income Per Share Diluted: Diluted net income per share was also $0.09, consistent with the basic earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Clearfield segment, which focuses on fiber management, protection, and delivery solutions, accounted for 86% of the company's total net sales for the three months ended March 31, 2025, and 85% for the six months ended March 31, 2025. The Nestor Cables segment, which designs and manufactures fiber optic and copper telecommunication cables, contributed 14% and 15% of total net sales for the same periods, respectively.

Geographical Performance: International sales represented 16% of total net sales for the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to 27% for the same period in 2024. The decrease in international sales was primarily driven by reduced demand in the Nestor Cables segment's European market.

Sales Units: The Clearfield segment experienced increased sales to Large Regional Service Provider customers by 255%, MSO customers by 53%, and Community Broadband customers by 12% for the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024.

New Production Launches: Nestor Cables expanded its manufacturing facility in Tabasalu, Estonia, from approximately 49,000 square feet to 115,000 square feet during the first quarter of fiscal 2025, enhancing its production capacity.

Future Outlook: The company anticipates that certain tariffs in effect as of May 9, 2025, will impact future financial results, although the extent is uncertain. Clearfield, Inc. is actively monitoring the situation and working to mitigate potential impacts on its business operations.

SEC Filing: Clearfield, Inc. [ CLFD ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: ALLIANT ENERGY CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Alliant Energy Corp, a leading energy company providing electric and natural gas services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational advancements, driven by strategic investments and favorable market conditions.

Financial Highlights

Alliant Energy Corp reported robust financial performance for the first quarter of 2025:

  • Total Revenues: $1,128 million, reflecting a 9% increase from the same period in 2024, primarily due to higher revenue requirements from capital investments and temperature impacts on sales.
  • Operating Income: $257 million, an increase from $222 million in 2024, driven by higher revenue requirements and temperature impacts, partially offset by higher depreciation and financing expenses.
  • Net Income: $213 million, up from $158 million in 2024, primarily due to higher revenue requirements and favorable temperature impacts on sales.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.83, compared to $0.62 in 2024, reflecting the increase in net income attributable to Alliant Energy common shareowners.

Business Highlights

Alliant Energy Corp's operational performance for the first quarter of 2025 includes several key developments:

  • Revenue Segments: Alliant Energy's electric utility revenues were $853 million, with IPL contributing $430 million and WPL contributing $423 million. Gas utility revenues totaled $240 million, with IPL and WPL contributing $118 million and $122 million, respectively.
  • Sales Units: Alliant Energy's retail electric sales volumes increased by 3% for the three months ended March 31, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024, primarily due to changes in temperatures and increased sales volumes at WPL’s commercial and industrial customers. Retail gas sales volumes increased by 18% for the same period, primarily due to changes in temperatures.
  • New Production Launches: WPL filed a certificate of authority application with the PSCW for approval to construct a 2 billion cubic feet liquified natural gas facility in Rock County, Wisconsin. Additionally, WPL received authorization to construct a 17.5 MW natural gas-fired EGU using RICE technology at the Riverside Energy Center.
  • Future Outlook: Over the next six years, Alliant Energy plans to develop and/or acquire new generation investments, including approximately 1,500 MW of new natural gas resources, 1,200 MW of new wind generation, and 800 MW of new energy storage. These plans are part of Alliant Energy's strategy to add flexibility with evolving load growth.
  • Geographical Performance: WPL has entered into an electric service agreement with a new customer planning to build a data center in Beaver Dam, Wisconsin, which is expected to increase WPL's load. IPL and WPL have executed electric service agreements with aggregate, maximum demands of approximately 2.1 gigawatts.
  • Environmental Matters: The EPA announced plans to reconsider various environmental regulations, including the Clean Air Act Sections 111(b) and 111(d), which could impact Alliant Energy's operations. The company is currently unable to predict the future outcome or impact of these matters.
  • Rate Matters: WPL filed a retail electric and gas rate review with the PSCW for the 2026/2027 forward-looking Test Period, requesting increases in annual rates for its retail electric and gas customers. A decision from the PSCW is expected by the end of 2025.

SEC Filing: ALLIANT ENERGY CORP [ LNT ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: SLR Investment Is Maintained at Outperform by Raymond James
ICE Canola Strengthens at Midday Friday
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--The ICE Futures canola market was stronger at midday Friday, underpinned by supportive technical signals and the need to ration demand.

Weekly Canadian canola exports of 184,500 tonnes were up eight per cent from the previous week, with 2024/25 year-to-date exports of 7.9 million tonnes. That's well ahead of the 4.6 million tonnes exported by the same time the previous year, according to Canadian Grain Commission data. The exports have already surpassed Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada's target for the crop year of 7.5 million tonnes.

Gains in the Chicago soy complex provided spillover support, although European rapeseed was softer on the day.

An estimated 23,000 canola contracts traded as of 12:06 p.m. ET.

Prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne at 12:06 p.m. ET:

 
           Price      Change 
Jul       709.00     up 8.10 
Nov       669.10     up 6.60 
Jan       676.60     up 6.30 
Mar       682.80     up 5.60

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: Canada Unemployment Rate Rises to Five-Month High in April — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart

OTTAWA--Canada's jobs market is showing signs of cracking under the strains of the trade war with the U.S., with the unemployment rate in April pushed to a five-month high and hiring all but grinding to a halt.

Employers in the country added a modest 7,400 jobs last month, Statistics Canada said Friday. That masks what would have been a roughly 30,000 drop in job numbers if it wasn't for a temporary lift in public-administration roles tied to activities around the recent federal election.

The advance was slightly stronger than the about 5,000 jobs economists expected to be added in April but did little to recover from March's sharp fall in employment and follows lackluster hiring in February. It means that on average Canada has shed about 8,000 jobs a month over the last three months, a sharp reversal from the close to 70,000 jobs being added monthly in the three months through January.

Canada's unemployment rate has now risen two months running, advancing 0.2 percentage point in April, to 6.9%. That matches the recent peak in November, which in turn was the highest since the start of 2022. Excluding the pandemic years, unemployment in the country was last at these levels in early 2017.

Labor-market weakness raises the odds the central bank could resume cutting interest rates as early as the next policy meeting in June, provided the fallout from the Trump administration's import tariffs and Ottawa's counter-levies don't cause a spike in inflation. Statistics Canada's labor force survey is the first major data for April, reflecting the U.S. tariffs imposed on imports of foreign vehicles early that month and the first rounds of U.S. levies the month before aimed at Canada and Mexico, and steel and aluminum.

"It doesn't take an archeological dig to realize this is a weak report," said Douglas Porter, chief economist at Bank of Montreal. "It shows that tariffs are already taking a material bite out of the economy."

Job losses in April were particularly steep in goods-producing industries, with a loss of 30,000 that was centered largely on manufacturing and the province of Ontario, the heart of the country's automotive industry. Chrysler-parent Stellantis and General Motors have each moved to lay off temporarily workers at plants in Ontario since President Trump's vehicle tariffs came into effect.

Manufacturing is expected to bear the brunt of the tariff war as demand in the U.S.--Canada's biggest export target by a wide margin--widens. Surveys such as S&P Global's manufacturing purchasing managers index point to freezes on hiring and job cuts. Canadian manufacturing employment has now fallen by 42,500 since January.

Services industries also are likely to feel the pinch as Canadian households rein in spending and counter-tariffs imposed by Ottawa lift some prices. Employment in April fell in wholesale and retail trade, dropping by roughly 27,000 after a similar-sized decline the month before, and now offsetting a strong increase seen in February.

Mitigating the losses, the finance, insurance, real-estate, rental-and-leasing sector continued an upward trend in employment from October. And jobs in public administration saw the first significant rise since last July, though most of the gains last month were in temporary work and were tied to the election.

Overall, the number of unemployed in Canada--those looking for work or on temporary layoff--rose 2.6% during April and was 13.9% higher than a year earlier. Statistics Canada's data suggests the struggle to find work is intensifying, with 61% of those who were unemployed in March still having no job in April, up from 57.3% in the same months last year.

"The economic outlook still remains highly contingent on the evolution of international trade risks but job openings have continued to edge lower, signaling more softening in hiring in the pipeline," Claire Fan, senior economist at Royal Bank of Canada, said.

Like many other economists, she expects slowing conditions will push the unemployment rate up to more than 7% this year. That in turn could provide a catalyst for the Bank of Canada to resume easing monetary policy, after it in April held its benchmark rate steady after seven cuts in a row.

In advance of the job numbers, Bank of Canada Senior Deputy Gov. Carolyn Rogers said the report was one of the pieces of data that would feed into the next rate decision. At the time of April's policy meeting, bank officials had no hard data around the effects of trade uncertainty and tariffs, only indications of consumer sentiment and a surveys of business, she said Thursday.

Charles St-Arnaud, chief economist at credit-union advocacy group Alberta Central, said the job report confirms tariffs are weighing on the labor market as extreme uncertainty dents business sentiment and hiring intentions. "The deterioration of the labor market may be signalling the start of a possible recession in Canada due to the U.S. tariffs," he said.

Economists anticipate the central bank's increased sensitivity to the labor market's struggles as an early sign of economic weakness will add to a conviction that the interest rate needs to move lower. Wage growth remains more elevated than during the pre-pandemic period, and still is hotter than consumer-price inflation, but economists expect that too will continue to cool in the coming months as labor demand cools.

Write to Robb M. Stewart robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: US, Europeans finalising 30-day Ukraine-Russia ceasefire proposal, says diplomatic source
data.description:
The United States and its European allies are finalising a proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine that if refused would see them jointly impose new sanctions on Russia, a French diplomatic source said on Friday.

U.S. President Donald Trump called on Thursday for a 30-day unconditional ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, warning that Washington and its partners would impose further sanctions if the ceasefire was not respected.

Ukraine has expressed readiness to accept the U.S. proposal. Russia has unilaterally declared a three-day ceasefire running from May 8-10 to coincide with the 80th anniversary of the end of World War Two.

"We're not completely with a finalised project, but we hope that we're at a moment of convergence," said the diplomatic source, speaking on condition of anonymity.

"What could happen in the coming hours and days, there could be an announcement of a ceasefire either of 30 days or compartmentalized, which is still being discussed."

The source said there were still discussions on whether to announce a unilateral ceasefire or to give a short response time to Russia, although if it refused then new American and EU sanctions would be imposed on Moscow.

The two sides are coordinating on the sanctions packages.

French Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot, who was in Washington last week to meet U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will join President Emmanuel Macron in Kyiv on Saturday, where there will be a hybrid meeting of Ukraine's closest allies and discussions on the U.S.-European proposal.

The source said political and technical talks between Europe and the U.S. had stepped up since last week. Trump and Macron spoke on Thursday to discuss the ceasefire proposal.

"We felt in the discussions with the Americans a certain irritation towards the Russian posture, the lack of reactivity and seriousness in its responses to what was proposed before," the source said. "The decision is practically taken."

data.title: Germany's new finance minister supports Commerzbank independence, DPA reports
data.description:
Germany's new finance minister Lars Klingbeil voiced opposition on Friday to a possible takeover of Commerzbank by Italy's UniCredit , the German news agency DPA reported.

"We are backing Commerzbank's independence," Klingbeil told DPA, echoing the stance of Germany's previous government.

"An unfriendly approach like that of UniCredit is unacceptable. This is especially true when it comes to a systemically important bank like Commerzbank."

data.title: Onto Innovation shares near 2-year low on weak quarterly forecasts
data.description:
** Shares in semiconductor equipment maker Onto Innovation Inc down 30.3% at $88.58, hitting lowest point since May 2023

** Co forecasts Q2 revenue in the range of $240 million-$260 million vs estimates of $263.7 mln, and Q2 adj. EPS between $1.21 and $1.35 vs estimates of $1.46 - LSEG

** Co reports Q1 profit and revenue slightly above analysts' expectations

** At least four brokerages cut PT on stock

** Brokerage Needham says that market share loss in AI packaging technology to competitor KLA Corporation "could mean the rapid derating of ONTO over the past three months is unlikely to reverse in the near term"

** Including session's losses, stock down ~46% YTD vs Philadelphia semiconductor index's decline of ~10% YTD

data.title: Class Action Filed Against Avis Budget Group, Inc. (CAR) Seeking Recovery for Investors - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 9, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Avis Budget Group, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/avis-budget-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147796&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Avis Budget Group, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between February 16, 2024 and February 10, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) Avis Budget crafted and implemented a plan to significantly accelerate its fleet rotation in the fourth quarter of 2024; (ii) the foregoing acceleration shortened the useful life of the majority of the Company's vehicles in the Americas segment, thereby reducing their recoverable value; (iii) as a result, Avis Budget would be forced to recognize billions of dollars in impairment charges and incur substantial losses; (iv) all the foregoing was likely to, and did, have a significant negative impact on the Company's financial results; (v) accordingly, Avis Budget's financial and/or business prospects were overstated; and (vi) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Avis Budget stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/avis-budget-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147796&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

Tel: (212) 363-7500

Fax: (212) 363-7171

https://zlk.com/

SOURCE: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Investors in Canopy Growth Corporation Should Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before June 3, 2025 to Discuss Your Rights - CGC
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 9, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Canopy Growth Corporation investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/canopy-growth-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147794&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1025997&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2fZkmnm9ov38g" width="0"/></div></div></div><p><strong>THE LAWSUIT:</strong> A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Canopy Growth Corporation that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 30, 2024 and February 6, 2025.</p><p><strong>CASE DETAILS:</strong> The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) Canopy had incurred significant costs producing Claybourne pre-rolled joints in connection with the Claybourne product launch in Canada; (ii) the foregoing costs, in addition to certain indirect costs that Canopy incurred in connection with its Storz & Bickel vaporizer devices, were likely to have a significant negative impact on the Company's gross margins and overall financial results; (iii) accordingly, defendants had overstated the efficacy of Canopy's cost reduction measures and the health of its gross margins while downplaying issues with the same; and (iv) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.</p><p><strong>WHAT'S NEXT?</strong> If you suffered a loss in Canopy Growth Corporation stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to <a href="https://pr.report/a9zj" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/pslra-1/canopy-growth-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147794&wire=1</a> to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. <strong>There is no cost or obligation to participate.</strong></p><p><strong>WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY:</strong> Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.</p><p><strong>CONTACT:</strong><br/>Levi & Korsinsky, LLP<br/>Joseph E. Levi, Esq.<br/>Ed Korsinsky, Esq.<br/>33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor<br/>New York, NY 10004<br/><a href="mailto:jlevi@levikorsinsky.com" rel="nofollow">jlevi@levikorsinsky.com</a><br/>Tel: (212) 363-7500<br/>Fax: (212) 363-7171<br/><a href="https://pr.report/a9zl" rel="nofollow">https://zlk.com/</a></p><p><strong>SOURCE:</strong> Levi & Korsinsky, LLP</p><br/><br/>View the original <a href="https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/business-and-professional-services/investors-in-canopy-growth-corporation-should-contact-levi-and-k-1025997">press release</a> on ACCESS Newswire<br/><br/><img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/img.ashx?id=1025997" width="0"/></body></html>

data.title: ATTENTION CODI SHAREHOLDERS: Investors Who Lost Money on Compass Diversified are Urged to Contact Levi & Korsinsky About an Ongoing Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 9, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Compass Diversified ("Compass Diversified") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On May 8, 2025, Compass announced that it intends to delay the filings of its first quarter 2025 Form 10-Q. The Company also disclosed non-reliance on its financial statements for fiscal 2024 amid an ongoing internal investigation into its subsidiary, Lugano Holding. Further, effective May 7, 2025 Lugano's founder and CEO, Moti Ferder, resigned from Lugano and will not receive any severance compensation.

Following this news, CODI's stock price fell by $8.75 per share to open at $8.50 per share. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/compass-diversified-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147793&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

SOURCE: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

data.title: Rumble to Launch Tether-Collab Crypto Wallet in Q3 2025. Here’s Why the Best Wallet Token Could Explode
data.description:
Chris Pavlovski, Rumble’s founder and CEO, took to X to announce the launch of a Bitcoin and stablecoin wallet.

As per Chris’s X post, we can expect to see the newest entrant in the Bitcoin wallets space by the third quarter of 2025. The Rumble CEO also mentioned that the wallet would directly compete with Coinbase.

Keep reading to learn more about Rumble’s latest crypto adventure. We’ll unpack all the details and why existing solutions like Best Wallet, whose native crypto, Best Wallet Token, already has over $12M in early investor funding, give Rumble very little breathing room.

Rumble-Tether Partnership

Rumble’s crypto wallet will be a collaborative effort between Rumble and stablecoin issuer Tether.

Pavlovski claims that the Rumble Wallet aims to boost the creator economy by ‘monetiz[ing] creators better than most advertisers, especially in international markets.’

Also, this isn’t Tether’s first date night with Rumble. The stablecoin giant invested a whopping $775M in Rumble in December 2024, following which Rumble announced the launch of a Tether wallet in March of this year.

Rumble Wallet’s Tough Road Ahead

There’s no debating just how crowded the Bitcoin wallet space is at the moment. Big-name providers like Coinbase, PayPal, Robinhood, and Revolut dominate the streets, and then we have upcoming projects like Best Wallet that are rapidly gaining momentum. Where does Rumble fit in all this?

Coinbase, for instance, recorded over 100M registered users in 2024 alone. This is nearly double the number of users on Rumble’s video platform, which is supposedly its bread and butter.

Even though it’s an uphill task, we believe Rumble would be very happy if it could just grab a teeny tiny bite of the growing mobile crypto wallet industry in its first few months.

With a prominent crypto player eyeing its wallet debut, this could be the perfect opportunity to look at what other new crypto wallets are offering.

We tested and reviewed dozens of these wallets and found Best Wallet to be the most worthy of your attention.

What Is Best Wallet?

Best Wallet is a new crypto wallet that has been making headlines thanks to its airtight privacy and class-leading ease of use. When compiling our detailed Best Wallet review, what impressed us the most was the free crypto wallet‘s mobile-friendly design.

We believe it’s an inspired idea for a new wallet to concentrate on perfecting its mobile app, as that means people can use it both at home and when on the go.

It’s worth noting, though, that Best Wallet is also developing browser extensions for Windows and macOS as we speak. Best Wallet Offers Top-Notch Security

Of course, we wouldn’t recommend a crypto wallet if it weren’t 100% secure, which Best Wallet is.

For starters, it’s a non-custodial wallet, meaning you get complete control over your private keys. In other words, you’ll be the only entity that can access your crypto stored on Best Wallet.

With ill-intended cybercriminals always on the lookout for vulnerable crypto storage – surely you remember Bybit’s $1.5B crypto hack – it helps to have a wallet that uses advanced cryptographic techniques.

In fact, Best Wallet is among the first in the industry to employ Fireblock’s MPC-CMP wallet technology. Combined with 2FA/biometrics for login security, you can rest assured that Best Wallet will look after your crypto wealth with utmost sincerity and care.

What’s more, Best Wallet also provides you with crypto insurance, which will make you whole in the unlikely event of your portfolio being affected by a hack or phishing attack.

In addition to class-leading security and ease of use, Best Wallet also impresses with a few never-before-seen features for crypto (and meme coin) investors.

It’s the ONLY crypto wallet that allows its users to purchase any of the new meme coins on presale directly from within the app.

Note that other wallets ask their users to take the long route, which involves visiting the token’s presale website, connecting their wallet account, and then approving the transaction from the wallet again. Phew!

Buying the top altcoins directly from Best Wallet is not just convenient, but it’s also more secure. That’s because the in-house Best Wallet team vets every single crypto before listing it on their app.

Ride Best Wallet’s Growth by Investing in the Best Wallet Token ($BEST)

It’s easy to conclude from the above that Best Wallet is destined for great things. In fact, the company predicts it can capture over 40% of the non-custodial crypto wallet market by 2026. If you want to join Best Wallet in its journey to redefine retail crypto storage solutions, become an early investor in Best Wallet Token ($BEST).

$BEST is the native cryptocurrency of the Best Wallet app, meaning it’ll offer you the best seat in the house if you wish to become a part of Best Wallet’s growth story.

It’s worth noting that according to our $BEST price prediction, the token could jump a staggering 280% and reach $0.07 by the end of 2030.

Don’t delay your purchase of the next crypto to explode and buy $BEST today. The project is in presale right now (with $12M+ raised), which is why you can grab it for the low price of just $0.024995.

For more technical information about the project, check out its whitepaper. We also suggest checking out its X feed and Telegram channel for regular updates.

To conclude, kindly bear in mind that investments in the crypto market are highly risky because of the broader market’s unpredictable volatility. Also, this article is not financial advice, and we urge you to do your own research before investing.
